LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF ANDRIY KRAVCHUK'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER OF SPECIAL REFERENCE APPOINTING JUDGE DAVID A. GARCIA (RET.) SPECIAL MASTER FOR THE PURPOSE OF HEARING AND ANY AND ALL DISCOVERY MOTIONS. THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFFS ANDRIY KRAVCHUK AND ARTEM KOSHKKALDA'S MOTION FOR ORDER OF SPECIAL REFERENCE APPOINTING JUDGE DAVID GARCIA IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO BEING REFILED IF THE PARTIES HAVE ADDITIONAL DISCOVERY DISPUTES BEYOND THOSE SET FORTH IN THE MOVING PAPERS. APPOINTMENT OF A DISCOVERY REFEREE OVER A PARTY'S OBJECTION REQUIRES "EXCEPTIONAL CIRCUMSTANCES." (CCP 639(D)(2)). EVEN ASSUMING THAT THE GREAT MAJORITY OF DEFENDANTS' 190 DISCOVERY REQUESTS WERE IMPROPER, THAT DOES NOT, WITHOUT MORE, ESTABLISH EXCEPTIONAL CIRCUMSTANCES. ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/HK)

DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR TERMINATING AND MONETARY SANCTIONS: UNDER THE DISCOVERY ACT, "THE COURT SHALL IMPOSE A MONETARY SANCTION . . . AGAINST ANY PARTY, PERSON, OR ATTORNEY WHO UNSUCCESSFULLY MAKES OR OPPOSES A MOTION TO COMPEL . . . UNLESS IT FINDS THAT THE ONE SUBJECT TO THE SANCTION ACTED WITH SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION OR THAT OTHER CIRCUMSTANCES MAKE THE IMPOSITION OF THE SANCTION UNJUST." CAL. CIV. PROC. CODE 2030.290(C), 2031.300(C), 2025.480(J); SEE ALSO CAL. CIV. PROC. CODE 2033.280. "SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION AS USED IN THE DISCOVERY STATUTES MEANS A JUSTIFICATION THAT IS WELL-GROUNDED IN BOTH LAW AND FACT." DIEPENBROCK V. BROWN, 208 CAL. APP. 4TH 743, 747 (2012) (CLEANED UP). "THE BURDEN OF PROVING SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION FOR FAILING TO COMPLY WITH A DISCOVERY ORDER COMPELLING ANSWERS OR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND OPPOSING A MOTION TO COMPEL COMPLIANCE IS ON THE LOSING PARTY CLAIMING THAT IT ACTED WITH SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION." DOE V. U.S. SWIMMING, INC., 200 CAL. APP. 4TH 1424, 1435 (2011) (CLEANED UP). PLAINTIFFS, REPRESENTING THEMSELVES IN PRO PER, AVER THAT THEY SENT THE DISCOVERY "PER COURT'S ORDER." DEFENDANTS' REPLY STATES THAT DEFENDANTS HAVE NOW RECEIVED THE DISCOVERY AT ISSUE. ACCORDINGLY, DEFENDANTS' MOTION IS DENIED. DEFENDANTS' REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS DENIED. ALL OTHER SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. AS THE PREVAILING PARTIES, DEFENDANTS SHALL SUBMIT A FORM OF THE ORDER RECAPITULATING THIS ADOPTED RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CAL. R. CT. 3.1312 VIA EMAIL TO NGRAY@SHADESOFGRAY.LAW JUDGE PRO TEM: NAOMI GRAY, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

Motion For A New Trial
Off Calendar Aug-27-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Plaintiff ARTEM KOSHKALDA (Tel: 669-236-9559) appearing in pro per;
Deputy City Attorney HUNTER W. SIMS III of the Office of the City Attorney of the City and County of San Francisco (Tel: 415-554-4259) appearing for defendants City and County of San Francisco and Omar Masry.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF ARTEM KOSHKALDA's Motion For A New Trial.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff Artem Koshkalda's motion for a new trial is denied. The court lacks jurisdiction to rule on the motion, which is untimely. (Code Civ. Proc., 659(a)(2) [notice of intent to move for new trial must be filed within 15 days after service of written notice of entry judgment]; Kabran v. Sharp Memorial Hospital (2017) 2 Cal.5th 330, 337 ["the trial court loses jurisdiction to hear a new trial motion if no notice of intent is filed within 15 days of the mailing or service of notice of entry of judgment"].) Judgment was entered on June 12, 2019 and notice of entry of judgment was served on plaintiff by electronic mail on June 19, 2019, but the motion was not filed until August 2, 2019. The court's July 30, 2019 order correcting a minor typographical error in its original judgment (regarding the date on which the City's memorandum of costs was filed) did not change the effect of the judgment or extend the time to file a motion for new trial. The court also notes that it does not appear that plaintiff has standing to pursue the matter. The court takes judicial notice that plaintiff filed a petition for relief under the Bankruptcy Code and scheduled the instant lawsuit as an asset of the bankruptcy estate, and that E. Lynn Schoenmann was appointed as plaintiff's Chapter 7 Trustee. Under the Bankruptcy Code, the Trustee administers property of the bankruptcy estate, including causes of action. (11 US.C. 704(a); Haley v. Dow Lewis Motors, Inc. (1999) 72 Cal.App.4th 497, 503-504 ["'It is of course indisputable that any causes of action which accrue to a debtor who has filed for relief under the Bankruptcy Act before the filing of the bankruptcy petition become the property of the bankruptcy estate and may thereafter be prosecuted only by the trustee or a duly appointed representative of the estate.'"].)

Order signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano, CSR#8600, e-mail: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)

Motion And Notice Of Hearing Re Plaintiff Motion To Amend Judgment Based On Clerical Error
Off Calendar Jul-30-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Plaintiff ARTEM KOSHKALDA (Tel: 669-236-9559) appearing in pro per;
Deputy City Attorney HUNTER W. SIMS III of the San Francisco Office of the City Attorney (Tel: 415-554-4259) appearing for defendant City and County of San Francisco.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF ARTEM KOSHKALDA'S Motion To Amend Judgment Based On Clerical Error.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff's motion to amend judgment based on clerical error is granted in part. The Court's June 12, 2019 Judgment contained a typographical error that referred to the filing date of the City's memorandum of costs as May 4, 2018 rather than May 6, 2019. Pursuant to the Court's inherent power to correct clerical errors in a judgment at any time, the judgment is amended to reflect the correct filing date. Plaintiff's motion is otherwise denied. Although the Court denied the City's motion for an award of attorneys' fees under Code of Civil Procedure section 1038, the City is independently entitled under section 1032(b) to recover its costs as a matter of right as a prevailing party. The record demonstrates that defendant filed and served the costs memorandum on May 6, 2019, and plaintiff never filed a motion to tax costs in compliance with California Rule of Court 3.1700, which provides that a notice of motion to strike or to tax costs must be served and filed 15 days after service of the cost memorandum. Plaintiff's failure to file a timely motion to tax costs waived any objections. (Santos v. Civil Service Bd. (1987) 193 Cal.App.3d 1442, 1447 ["failure to file a motion to tax costs constitutes a waiver of the right to object"].) Plaintiff's reference to costs in his brief in opposition to the City's fees motion did not relieve him of the obligation to comply with Rule 3.1700.

Order signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: Sean Kane, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185, E-MAIL: tonyvaughn@yahoo.com (302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating And Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-25-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No other appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion For Terminating And Monetary Sanctions

Under the Discovery Act, "the court shall impose a monetary sanction . . . against any party, person, or attorney who unsuccessfully makes or opposes a motion to compel . . . unless it finds that the one subject to the sanction acted with substantial justification or that other circumstances make the imposition of the sanction unjust." Cal. Civ. Proc. Code 2030.290(c), 2031.300(c), 2025.480(j); see also Cal. Civ. Proc. Code 2033.280. "Substantial justification as used in the discovery statutes means a justification that is well-grounded in both law and fact." Diepenbrock v. Brown, 208 Cal. App. 4th 743, 747 (2012) (cleaned up). "The burden of proving substantial justification for failing to comply with a discovery order compelling answers or production of documents and opposing a motion to compel compliance is on the losing party claiming that it acted with substantial justification." Doe v. U.S. Swimming, Inc., 200 Cal. App. 4th 1424, 1435 (2011) (cleaned up). Plaintiffs, representing themselves in pro per, aver that they sent the discovery "per Court's order." Defendants' reply states that Defendants have now received the discovery at issue. Accordingly, Defendants' motion is DENIED. Defendants' request for monetary sanctions is DENIED. All other sanctions are DENIED.

As the prevailing parties, Defendants shall submit a form of the order recapitulating this adopted ruling in compliance with Cal. R. Ct. 3.1312 via email to ngray@shadesofgray.law

Judge Pro Tem: Naomi Gray, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash The Defendants Subpoena For Production Of Business Records Directed To Airbnb, Inc.
Off Calendar Apr-02-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Artem Koshkalda, 669-236-9559 Plaintiff in Pro Per.
Hunter Sims, III Deputy City Attorney, 415-554-4259 for Defendant City and County of San Francisco.

Both sides stipulate to the Judge Pro Tem.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF ANDRIY KRAVCHUK, ARTEM KOSHKALDA's Motion To Quash The Defendants Subpoena For Production Of Business Records Directed To Airbnb, Inc.

The motion to quash is DENIED. First, the notice of motion was not compliant with CCP section 1005 (insufficient notice). Second, movants did not meet and confer. Third, on the merits, the subpoena seeks records related to business transactions that are relevant to, or reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of information and potential witnesses relevant to, the claims and defenses of the parties. CCP 2017.010. The Court does not find the privacy arguments well taken because the subpoena seeks records related to commercial transactions. AirBNB was served with the subpoena and did not object, so the Court declines to speculate as to any burden to be borne by AirBNB. Whether the evidence obtained will be admissible is not the question on whether it is discoverable. Williams v. Sup. Ct. (2017) 3 Cal.5th 531, 541.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge Pro Tem: Scott Borrowman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-16-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Alex Gortinsky(916-961-5696) for Plaintiff/Opposing party Andriy Kravchuk via Court Call.
Newton Oldfather(415-554-4283) for Defendant/Moving party CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, and OMAR MASRY .

ARGUED re Sham Pleadings. Plaintiff will amend complaint. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, OMAR MASRY DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is
SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer to the second cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. Non-opposition filed. Demurrer to the first cause of action for defamation is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts addressing the changed allegations in the first amended complaint. Compare Complaint paragraph 21 and First Amended Complaint paragraph 11. The Court reserves the ruling on the issue of immunity and privilege until such time when the "sham pleading" issue is resolved. The demurrer regarding the sufficiency of allegations regarding defamation is overruled. Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: S. Le; J. Lee Not Reported. (D501)

Order To Show Cause Why A Preliminary Injuction Should Not Be Granted
Off Calendar Oct-03-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFFS' ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE WHY A PRELIMINARY INJUCTION SHOULD NOT BE GRANTED

Appearances: Newton Oldfather of the Office of the City Attorney (415.554.4283) for defendant City and County of San Francisco; No other appearances.

This matter is not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DENIED. Moving party failed to carry its burden and show a likelihood of prevailing on the merits, i.e. there is no evidence that Plaintiff followed the proper procedure to challenge the imposition of fines and there is no connection between the fined and defamation claims. Further, moving party has a remedy at law. Order signed. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/REQ)
